doocs/cose: One-Click Markdown Publishing to 13+ Content Platforms

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

doocs/cose: One-Click Markdown Publishing to 13+ Content Platforms

Section titled “doocs/cose: One-Click Markdown Publishing to 13+ Content Platforms”

“Write once, publish everywhere” - the dream of every content creator.

If you’re a technical writer, developer, or content creator who publishes across multiple platforms, you know the pain of copying and pasting your content to each platform individually. Each platform has its own editor, formatting quirks, and login flow. It’s tedious, error-prone, and wastes valuable time.

doocs/cose solves this problem elegantly. As a browser extension designed for the popular doocs/md Markdown editor, cose enables one-click synchronization of your articles to 13+ content platforms simultaneously.

Introduction and Key Features

Section titled “Introduction and Key Features”

doocs/cose (short for “Content One-click SynchronizE”) is a browser extension developed by Yang Shuang (lead1314) that integrates seamlessly with the doocs/md Markdown editor. Its mission is simple: eliminate the friction of multi-platform content publishing.

Core Philosophy

Section titled “Core Philosophy”
  • 100% Local Operation - No data collection, no cloud storage, complete privacy
  • Write Once, Publish Everywhere - Edit in Markdown, sync to all platforms
  • Automatic Tab Management - Synced tabs are grouped for easy organization
  • Style Preservation - Especially for WeChat Official Account with full rendering

Supported Platforms (13+)

Section titled “Supported Platforms (13+)”
CategoryPlatforms
Social MediaWeChat Official Account, Zhihu, Jianshu
Tech CommunitiesCSDN, Juejin, SegmentFault (思否), OSChina
Media PlatformsToutiao (今日头条), Baijiahao (百家号), NetEase (网易号)
EnterpriseTencent Cloud (腾讯云), 51CTO, InfoQ
TraditionalCNBlogs (博客园)

Prerequisites, Installation and Configuration

Section titled “Prerequisites, Installation and Configuration”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”

Before installing cose, ensure you have:

  1. Google Chrome or a Chromium-based browser (Brave, Edge, etc.)
  2. Active accounts on the platforms you want to publish to
  3. doocs/md Markdown Editor - either the web version at md.doocs.org or a local development environment

Installation Methods

Section titled “Installation Methods”

Method 1: Chrome Web Store (Recommended)

Section titled “Method 1: Chrome Web Store (Recommended)”
  1. Visit the Chrome Web Store
  2. Search for “doocs/cose” or navigate directly from the GitHub repository
  3. Click “Add to Chrome”
  4. Grant necessary permissions

Method 2: Developer Mode

Section titled “Method 2: Developer Mode”

For testing the latest version or contributing:

First-Time Setup

Section titled “First-Time Setup”
  1. Install the Extension following one of the methods above
  2. Open doocs/md at md.doocs.org or your local instance
  3. Login to Platforms - Open each platform you want to publish to and login
  4. Grant Permissions - The extension will request necessary permissions on first use

Recommended Scenarios and Best Practices

Section titled “Recommended Scenarios and Best Practices”

Daily Workflow for Content Creators

Section titled “Daily Workflow for Content Creators”

Step 1: Write in Markdown

Section titled “Step 1: Write in Markdown”

Use doocs/md editor to craft your content:

Step 2: Preview and Refine

Section titled “Step 2: Preview and Refine”

The doocs/md editor provides real-time preview with:

  • Live rendering as you type
  • WeChat style preview for accurate formatting
  • Syntax highlighting for code blocks
  • Table editor for easy table creation

Step 3: One-Click Publish

Section titled “Step 3: One-Click Publish”
  1. Click the “Publish” (发布) button at the top of the editor
  2. A dialog appears showing all supported platforms
  3. Select the platforms you want to publish to
  4. Click “Confirm” (确定) to start synchronization

Step 4: Review and Manage

Section titled “Step 4: Review and Manage”
  • Tab Grouping: Synced tabs are automatically grouped
  • Login Detection: Extension shows which platforms you’re logged into
  • Draft Saving: WeChat articles are saved as drafts with full styling

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

  1. Platform-Specific Adjustments

    • Some platforms have title length limits
    • Image hosting varies (use external hosting for better compatibility)
    • Tags/keywords work differently per platform

  2. Content Organization

    • Use clear heading structures (H1, H2, H3)
    • Keep paragraphs concise for mobile readers
    • Test code blocks on platforms that support syntax highlighting

  3. WeChat Special Handling

    • cose preserves full rendering styles for WeChat
    • Articles are saved as drafts (not auto-published)
    • Review in WeChat editor before final publication

  4. Batch Publishing

    • Select multiple platforms at once
    • The extension handles each platform’s API differently
    • Monitor each tab for completion status

Common Use Cases

Section titled “Common Use Cases”
Use CaseRecommended Workflow
Technical BloggingWrite once, publish to CSDN, Juejin, Zhihu, CNBlogs
WeChat Official AccountUse doocs/md for writing, sync as draft, review in WeChat editor
Content DistributionCreate on personal blog, distribute to 10+ platforms simultaneously
DocumentationWrite technical docs, publish to Tencent Cloud, InfoQ, OSChina

Precautions, FAQ, and Troubleshooting

Section titled “Precautions, FAQ, and Troubleshooting”

Precautions

Section titled “Precautions”
  1. Login Status: Ensure you’re logged into each platform before syncing
  2. Content Format: Some platforms may strip certain Markdown features
  3. Image Hosting: Use external image hosting (not local file references)
  4. Rate Limits: Some platforms may have anti-spam mechanisms
  5. Draft Review: Always review synced content, especially for WeChat

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: Is my content secure? A: Yes. The extension operates 100% locally. No data is collected or stored on external servers.

Q: Why did my sync fail on Platform X? A: Common reasons include: not logged in, network issues, platform API changes, or content format restrictions. Check the browser console for error details.

Q: Can I sync to platforms not listed? A: The project welcomes contributions. Submit an Issue or PR for new platform support.

Q: Does it work with local doocs/md? A: Yes, the extension works with both the web version at md.doocs.org and local development environments.

Q: What happens to code blocks? A: cose attempts to preserve code formatting, but syntax highlighting support varies by platform. Test on your target platforms first.

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”
IssueSolution
Extension not loadingCheck Developer Mode is enabled, reload extension
Login not detectedRefresh the platform page, ensure cookies are enabled
Sync button missingEnsure you’re on md.doocs.org with a valid Markdown document
WeChat formatting brokenCheck for unsupported CSS features, review in WeChat editor
Partial sync failureSome platforms may have specific content restrictions

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”

doocs/cose vs. Traditional Copy-Paste

Section titled “doocs/cose vs. Traditional Copy-Paste”
Featuredoocs/coseCopy-Paste
Platforms13+ simultaneous1 at a time
Time per Article~2 minutes30+ minutes
FormattingPreservedManual cleanup needed
ImagesAutomatic handlingManual re-upload
Privacy100% localVaries

doocs/cose vs. Other Multi-Platform Tools

Section titled “doocs/cose vs. Other Multi-Platform Tools”
ToolPlatformsPrivacyPricing
doocs/cose13+100% localFree (Open Source)
Browser-based CMSVariesCloud-basedPaid subscriptions
Manual APIsUnlimitedSelf-hostedDevelopment cost
WordPress pluginsLimitedSelf-hostedFree/Paid

Why Choose doocs/cose?

Section titled “Why Choose doocs/cose?”
  1. Open Source & Transparent - Fully auditable code, community-driven
  2. Privacy-First - No data leaves your browser
  3. Free Forever - MIT licensed, no hidden costs
  4. Active Development - Regular updates, responsive to issues
  5. Platform-Specific Optimization - Each platform’s quirks are handled individually

Integration with doocs/md Ecosystem

Section titled “Integration with doocs/md Ecosystem”

The doocs project provides several complementary tools:

  • doocs/md - The core Markdown editor with WeChat styling
  • doocs/cose - Multi-platform publishing extension
  • doocs/decode - QR code and barcode tool

This integrated ecosystem ensures a smooth workflow from writing to publishing.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”

Official Resources

Section titled “Official Resources”
  • GitHub Repository: https://github.com/doocs/cose
  • doocs/md Editor: https://md.doocs.org
  • Chrome Web Store: Available (search “doocs/cose”)
  • Issues & Support: GitHub Issues

Related Projects

Section titled “Related Projects”
  • doocs/md: https://github.com/doocs/md
  • WeChat Markdown Editor: https://md.doocs.org

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

The project actively welcomes contributions:

  • Report bugs via Issues
  • Submit platform support requests
  • Contribute code via Pull Requests
  • Star the repository to support development

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

doocs/cose represents a significant productivity boost for content creators who need to maintain presence across multiple platforms. By combining the simplicity of Markdown editing with powerful one-click publishing, it frees creators to focus on what matters most: creating great content.

Whether you’re a technical blogger, documentation writer, or content marketer, cose eliminates the friction of multi-platform publishing. Its commitment to privacy (100% local operation), open-source ethos, and active community make it a valuable addition to any creator’s toolkit.

Try it today: Install the extension, open doocs/md, and experience the joy of writing once and publishing everywhere.

Tags:
  • Developer Tools
  • Markdown
  • Content Publishing
  • Browser Extension
  • Open Source

Eigent AI: The World's First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Eigent AI: The World’s First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform

Section titled “Eigent AI: The World’s First Multi-Agent Workforce Platform”

Introduction: The New Productivity Engine

Section titled “Introduction: The New Productivity Engine”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI automation, Eigent emerges as the world’s first Multi-Agent Workforce desktop application. Built on the robust foundation of CAMEL-AI, Eigent empowers users to build, manage, and deploy a custom AI workforce that transforms complex workflows into automated tasks.

Build your own AI Agent Workforce with Eigent — a non-intrusive integration that offers adaptable, ever-improving performance for any scenario, letting AI Workers run your operations automatically.

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

🏭 Multi-Agent Workforce

Section titled “🏭 Multi-Agent Workforce”

Eigent employs a team of specialized AI agents that collaborate to solve complex tasks. The platform dynamically breaks down tasks and activates multiple agents to work in parallel, significantly boosting productivity through coordinated effort.

Pre-defined Agent Workers:

  • Developer Agent: Writes and executes code, runs terminal commands
  • Search Agent: Searches the web and extracts content
  • Document Agent: Creates and manages documents
  • Multi-Modal Agent: Processes images and audio

🔌 MCP Tools Integration

Section titled “🔌 MCP Tools Integration”

Eigent comes with massive built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools and allows you to install your own custom tools. Equip agents with exactly the right tools for your scenarios — even integrate internal APIs or custom functions.

Built-in MCP Tools Include:

  • Web browsing capabilities
  • Code execution environments
  • Notion integration
  • Google Suite connectivity
  • Slack integration
  • And many more…

🎯 Infinitely Customizable

Section titled “🎯 Infinitely Customizable”

Build your own worker nodes to execute any task. With pluggable MCPs, you have full control to create agents with the perfect tools for the job. Your workforce, your rules.

🔒 Private and Secure by Design

Section titled “🔒 Private and Secure by Design”

Your data stays yours, always. With a focus on local, private deployment, your workflows and sensitive information never leave your control. Eigent is open-source for full transparency and trust.

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

☁️ Cloud Version

Section titled “☁️ Cloud Version”

The fastest way to experience Eigent’s multi-agent AI capabilities is through their cloud platform:

  • Instant Access - Start building multi-agent workflows in minutes
  • Managed Infrastructure - They handle scaling, updates, and maintenance
  • Premium Support - Subscribe and get priority assistance from the engineering team

Get started at Eigent.ai →

🏠 Self-Hosting (Community Edition)

Section titled “🏠 Self-Hosting (Community Edition)”

For users who prefer local control, data privacy, or customization:

Prerequisites:

  • Node.js (version 18-22) and npm

Benefits of Self-Hosting:

  • Data Privacy - Keep sensitive data within your infrastructure
  • Customization - Modify and extend the platform to fit your needs
  • Cost Control - Avoid recurring cloud fees for large-scale deployments

🏢 Enterprise

Section titled “🏢 Enterprise”

For organizations requiring maximum security, customization, and control:

  • Commercial License available
  • Exclusive Features (like SSO & custom development)
  • Scalable Enterprise Deployment
  • Negotiated SLAs & implementation services

Contact: info@eigent.ai

Real-World Use Cases

Section titled “Real-World Use Cases”

1. Travel Planning

Section titled “1. Travel Planning”

Prompt: “We are two tennis fans and want to go see the tennis tournament in Palm Springs 2026. I live in SF - please prepare a detailed itinerary with flights, hotels, things to do for 3 days - around the time semifinal/finals are happening. We like hiking, vegan food and spas. Our budget is $5K.”

Eigent successfully planned a detailed 3-day tennis trip from SF to Palm Springs with flights, hotels, vegan food, hiking, spas, and finals tickets — all within a $5K budget, ending with a report + Slack message.

2. Financial Reporting

Section titled “2. Financial Reporting”

Prompt: “Please help me prepare a Q2 financial statement based on my bank transfer record file bank_transaction.csv in my desktop to a html report with chart to investors how much we have spent.”

Eigent processed CSV bank data and created an investor-ready HTML report with Q2 spending summary and charts.

3. Market Research

Section titled “3. Market Research”

Prompt: “Analyze the UK healthcare industry to support the planning of my next company. Provide a comprehensive market overview, including current trends, growth projections, and relevant regulations.”

Eigent delivered comprehensive market analysis with trends, regulations, growth projections, and identified top opportunities in professional HTML format.

4. Social Media Automation

Section titled “4. Social Media Automation”

Using customized Twitter MCP and Twitter Agent powered by open-source self-deployed models to help get information from public online posts then send tweets automatically.

5. Data Extraction

Section titled “5. Data Extraction”

Eigent collected all stargazers of the eigent-ai/eigent repository and built a table containing each stargazer’s GitHub username, name, GitHub profile URL, and public LinkedIn and X (Twitter) links found on their GitHub profile pages.

Tech Stack

Section titled “Tech Stack”

Backend

Section titled “Backend”
  • Framework: FastAPI
  • Package Manager: uv
  • Async Server: Uvicorn
  • Authentication: OAuth 2.0, Passlib
  • Multi-agent framework: CAMEL

Frontend

Section titled “Frontend”
  • Framework: React
  • Desktop App Framework: Electron
  • Language: TypeScript
  • UI: Tailwind CSS, Radix UI, Lucide React, Framer Motion
  • State Management: Zustand
  • Flow Editor: React Flow

Development Roadmap

Section titled “Development Roadmap”
TopicsDescriptionDiscord Channel
Context EngineeringPrompt caching, System prompt optimize, Toolkit docstring optimize, Context compressionJoin Discord
Multi-modal EnhancementMore accurate image understanding when using browser, Advanced video generationJoin Discord
Multi-agent systemWorkforce support fixed workflow, Workforce support multi-round conversionJoin Discord
Browser ToolkitBrowseCamp integration, Benchmark improvement, Forbid repeated page visiting, Automatic cache button clickingJoin Discord
Document ToolkitSupport dynamic file editingJoin Discord
Terminal ToolkitBenchmark improvement, Terminal-Bench integrationJoin Discord
Environment & RLEnvironment design, Data-generation, RL framework integration (VERL, TRL, OpenRLHF)Join Discord

Why Choose Eigent?

Section titled “Why Choose Eigent?”

✅ Zero Setup

Section titled “✅ Zero Setup”

No technical configuration required — download and start building your AI workforce.

✅ Multi-Agent Coordination

Section titled “✅ Multi-Agent Coordination”

Handle complex multi-agent workflows with intelligent task distribution.

✅ Enterprise Features

Section titled “✅ Enterprise Features”

SSO/Access control for organizational deployment.

✅ Local Deployment

Section titled “✅ Local Deployment”

Keep your data private with full local deployment capabilities.

✅ 100% Open Source

Section titled “✅ 100% Open Source”

Full transparency — download, inspect, and modify the code.

✅ Custom Model Support

Section titled “✅ Custom Model Support”

Deploy with your preferred models, whether cloud or local.

✅ MCP Integration

Section titled “✅ MCP Integration”

Massive ecosystem of tools with custom tool installation support.

Community & Support

Section titled “Community & Support”
  • GitHub: eigent-ai/eigent — Report bugs, request features, and track development
  • Discord: Join Discord — Get real-time support, chat with the community
  • X (Twitter): @Eigent_AI — Follow for updates, AI insights, and announcements
  • Documentation: docs.eigent.ai — Comprehensive guides and API reference

Licensing

Section titled “Licensing”

Eigent is licensed under the Eigent Open Source License, based on Apache 2.0 with additional conditions. For enterprise licensing inquiries, contact info@eigent.ai.

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Eigent represents a paradigm shift in AI automation — moving beyond single-agent chatbots to a true multi-agent workforce platform. Whether you’re an individual looking to automate personal workflows or an enterprise seeking scalable AI solutions, Eigent provides the tools, flexibility, and security to build your perfect AI workforce.

Liked this article? Follow for more insights on AI automation, multi-agent systems, and the future of work.

References:

  • Eigent Official Website
  • Eigent GitHub Repository
  • CAMEL-AI Framework
Tags:
  • multi-agent
  • AI-workforce
  • CAMEL-AI
  • MCP
  • automation

Liquid AI Cookbook: Examples, Tutorials and Apps Built with LFM Models

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

As artificial intelligence continues to evolve, the demand for efficient, deployable models that can run on edge devices has grown significantly. Liquid AI has emerged as a key player in this space with their open-weight LFM (Liquid Foundation Models) and the open-source LEAP SDK.

The Liquid AI Cookbook is a comprehensive repository containing examples, tutorials, and applications built with these technologies. Whether you’re looking to customize models, deploy to laptops and edge devices, or build complete applications, this cookbook provides the resources to get started.

What is Liquid AI?

Section titled “What is Liquid AI?”

Liquid AI is developing a new generation of hybrid models designed specifically for on-device deployment. Their LFM2 models range from 350M to 8B parameters, making them ideal for edge deployment while maintaining strong performance on agentic tasks, data extraction, RAG, creative writing, and multi-turn conversations.

Key Characteristics

Section titled “Key Characteristics”
  • Hybrid Architecture: Combines different modeling techniques for efficiency
  • On-Device First: Designed from the ground up for edge deployment
  • Specialized Models: Task-specific checkpoints for extraction, RAG, tool use, and math
  • Multi-Modal Support: Vision-language models (LFM2-VL) for visual understanding
  • Edge SDK: Native framework for mobile deployment (Android/iOS)

LFM2 Model Family

Section titled “LFM2 Model Family”

Text-to-Text Models

Section titled “Text-to-Text Models”

The LFM2 series is designed for agentic tasks and on-device deployment:

ModelSizeBest For
LFM2-8B-A1B8BComplex reasoning, multi-turn conversations
LFM2-2.6B2.6BBalanced performance and efficiency
LFM2-1.2B1.2BMobile deployment, real-time applications
LFM2-700M700MResource-constrained environments
LFM2-350M350MUltra-low latency scenarios

Specialized Nano Models

Section titled “Specialized Nano Models”

For specific use cases, Liquid AI offers specialized “nano” checkpoints:

ModelUse Cases
LFM2-1.2B-ExtractData extraction from unstructured text (emails, filings, tickets)
LFM2-1.2B-RAGRAG applications, chatbots, knowledge base Q&A
LFM2-1.2B-ToolReal-time API calls, database queries, system integrations
LFM2-350M-MathMathematical problem solving and reasoning

Vision-Language Models

Section titled “Vision-Language Models”
ModelSizeDescription
LFM2-VL-1.6B1.6BGeneral vision-language tasks
LFM2-VL-450M450MLightweight visual understanding

Example Applications

Section titled “Example Applications”

The cookbook includes several ready-to-run examples:

Invoice Parser

Section titled “Invoice Parser”

A Python CLI that extracts structured data from invoice images using a 2-step agentic workflow. Perfect for automating document processing tasks.

Audio Transcription CLI

Section titled “Audio Transcription CLI”

Real-time audio-to-text transcription using LFM2-Audio-1.5B with llama.cpp integration. Ideal for meeting notes, podcast transcripts, and voice memos.

LEAP Edge SDK

Section titled “LEAP Edge SDK”

The LEAP Edge SDK is Liquid AI’s native framework for running LFM2 models on mobile devices. It provides:

  • Native Support: Android (Kotlin) and iOS (Swift)
  • Simple API: As easy as calling cloud LLM endpoints
  • Real-Time Streaming: Token streaming for responsive chat experiences
  • Persistent History: Built-in conversation management

Android Examples

Section titled “Android Examples”
ExampleDescription
LeapChatComprehensive chat app with streaming, history, and modern UI
SloganAppSingle-turn text generation for marketing
ShareAIWebsite summary generator
Recipe GeneratorStructured output generation demo
VLMExampleVision-language model integration

iOS Examples

Section titled “iOS Examples”
ExampleDescription
LeapChatFull-featured chat with streaming and conversation management
LeapSloganExampleBasic SwiftUI text generation demo
Recipe GeneratorStructured output for iOS
Audio DemoAudio input/output with on-device inference

End-to-End Tutorials

Section titled “End-to-End Tutorials”

The cookbook provides complete tutorials from setup to deployment:

TutorialFocus
Image Classification with Local VLMsSuper fast, accurate image classification on edge
Chess Game with Small LLMsBuilding a chess game using local language models

Community Projects

Section titled “Community Projects”

Projects built by the community using Liquid AI models:

ProjectDescription
TranslatorLensOffline translation camera
Food Images Fine-tuningFine-tuning example for food recognition
Meeting Intelligence CLIMeeting summarization tool
Private Doc Q&AOn-device document Q&A with RAG

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
  • Python 3.8+ for Python-based examples
  • Android Studio for Android development
  • Xcode for iOS development
  • Basic understanding of ML concepts (helpful but not required)

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

Model Access

Section titled “Model Access”

Models are available on Hugging Face:

  • 🤗 Liquid AI on Hugging Face

Use Cases & Best Practices

Section titled “Use Cases & Best Practices”

When to Use LFM2 Models

Section titled “When to Use LFM2 Models”

Best for:

  • Agentic tasks and workflows
  • Data extraction and transformation
  • RAG applications
  • Tool use and function calling
  • Math reasoning (with specialized models)
  • On-device deployment
  • Mobile and edge applications

Not recommended for:

  • Knowledge-intensive tasks requiring massive training data
  • Complex programming tasks
  • Tasks better suited for larger cloud-based models

Deployment Considerations

Section titled “Deployment Considerations”

Mobile/Edge:

  • Start with 1.2B or smaller for mobile devices
  • Use specialized models (Extract, RAG, Tool) for specific tasks
  • Consider the trade-off between model size and response quality

Desktop/Laptop:

  • 2.6B to 8B models provide good performance
  • More RAM allows for larger context windows
  • Consider GPU acceleration for better performance

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
AspectLiquid AI (LFM2)Cloud LLMsOther Local Models
PrivacyFull local controlServer processingVaries
LatencyLow (local)Network-dependentLow (local)
CostFree (open weights)Per-token pricingVaries
OfflineYesNoYes
Mobile SupportNative SDKNoLimited
Model Sizes350M-8BVariesVaries

Limitations & Considerations

Section titled “Limitations & Considerations”

Current Limitations

Section titled “Current Limitations”
  • Knowledge Cutoff: Models may not have the most recent information
  • Programming: Not optimized for complex code generation
  • Niche Tasks: General-purpose models may struggle with highly specialized domains

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Choose the Right Model Size: Match model size to your hardware constraints
  2. Use Specialized Models: Leverage nano models for specific tasks
  3. Test Thoroughly: Validate performance on your specific use case
  4. Monitor Resources: Keep an eye on memory and CPU usage on edge devices

Support & Community

Section titled “Support & Community”
  • 📖 Liquid AI Documentation
  • 💬 Discord Community
  • 🤗 Hugging Face Organization
  • 🚀 Liquid Edge AI Platform

Contributing

Section titled “Contributing”

The cookbook welcomes contributions! If you’ve built something with Liquid AI models:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Add your project to the “Examples built by our community” section
  3. Open a PR with a link to your GitHub repo

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

The Liquid AI Cookbook represents an exciting development in the world of on-device AI. By providing open-weight models optimized for edge deployment, along with comprehensive examples and native mobile SDKs, Liquid AI is making it easier than ever to build AI applications that respect user privacy while delivering responsive experiences.

Whether you’re building a mobile app that needs local inference, creating an edge device solution, or simply want to experiment with efficient language models, the Liquid AI Cookbook offers the resources and examples to get started quickly.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • Official Cookbook: github.com/Liquid4All/cookbook
  • Documentation: docs.liquid.ai
  • Hugging Face: huggingface.co/LiquidAI
  • LEAP Platform: leap.liquid.ai
  • Discord: Join the community
  • License: Open Source (varies by component)
Tags:
  • AI Tools
  • Machine Learning
  • Edge AI
  • Open Source
  • Mobile Development
  • On-Device AI

llm-madness: A Lightweight Pipeline for Training Small GPT Models with Web UI

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

llm-madness: A Lightweight Pipeline for Training Small GPT Models with Web UI

Section titled “llm-madness: A Lightweight Pipeline for Training Small GPT Models with Web UI”

Training a custom GPT model from scratch typically requires complex infrastructure, expensive cloud resources, and specialized tooling. MaxHastings’ llm-madness inverts this assumption by providing a lightweight, end-to-end pipeline for training small GPT models locally, complete with a web-based UI for experiment management.

Introduction and Key Features

Section titled “Introduction and Key Features”

llm-madness is a Python-based toolkit that brings GPT model training within reach of individual developers and researchers. It provides everything needed to train custom language models: from BPE tokenizer training to model architecture design, from dataset management to run inspection—all through an intuitive web interface.

What Makes llm-madness Different

Section titled “What Makes llm-madness Different”
FeatureDescription
Complete PipelineData → Tokenizer → Tokenization → Training → Inspection
Web-Based UIVisual interface for configs, datasets, and run analysis
GPT ArchitectureClean, readable transformer implementation
Local-FirstNo cloud accounts or external services required
Experiment TrackingBuilt-in manifest system with provenance tracking

Core Capabilities

Section titled “Core Capabilities”
  1. Tokenizer Training - Configure BPE settings and build vocabularies from your own datasets
  2. Model Architecture - Design GPT-style transformers with configurable dimensions
  3. Dataset Management - Combine text files into versioned snapshots with SHA-256 hashing
  4. Training Infrastructure - AdamW optimizer with warmup, cosine decay, and checkpointing
  5. Run Inspection - Visualize loss curves, attention patterns, and token predictions
  6. Pipeline Orchestration - Chain stages together with automatic dependency resolution

Prerequisites, Installation and Configuration

Section titled “Prerequisites, Installation and Configuration”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
  • Python 3.8+ with pip
  • PyTorch for model training
  • (Optional) CUDA/MPS for GPU acceleration

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Quick Start

Section titled “Quick Start”

The web UI will automatically load and display your latest training run.

Project Structure

Section titled “Project Structure”

Recommended Scenarios and Best Practices

Section titled “Recommended Scenarios and Best Practices”

Ideal Use Cases

Section titled “Ideal Use Cases”

1. Educational Learning

Understand transformer internals with a clean, readable implementation:

  • Visualize attention patterns per layer and head
  • Inspect token-level predictions during training
  • Debug model behavior with trace mode

2. Rapid Prototyping

Validate ideas quickly without infrastructure overhead:

  • Proof-of-concept models for specific tasks
  • Synthetic data experiments
  • Baseline establishment for comparison

3. Domain-Specific Tokenization

Train custom tokenizers on specialized corpora:

  • Code repositories for programming languages
  • Medical or legal texts
  • Mathematical notation
  • Any specialized vocabulary

4. Architecture Research

Experiment with model configurations:

  • Layer and head count variations
  • Embedding dimension tuning
  • Modern variants (RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE)

Daily Workflow Example

Section titled “Daily Workflow Example”

Here’s a typical session with llm-madness:

Step 1: Prepare Data

Section titled “Step 1: Prepare Data”

Step 2: Launch Web UI

Section titled “Step 2: Launch Web UI”

Step 3: Create Dataset Snapshot

Section titled “Step 3: Create Dataset Snapshot”

Use the web UI to create a versioned snapshot from your text file. The snapshot includes:

  • SHA-256 hash for reproducibility
  • Automatic train/validation split
  • Metadata tracking

Step 4: Train Tokenizer

Section titled “Step 4: Train Tokenizer”

Configure BPE settings in the web UI:

  • Vocabulary size
  • Special token patterns
  • Pre-tokenization options

Step 5: Configure Model Architecture

Section titled “Step 5: Configure Model Architecture”

Design your GPT model:

  • Number of layers and attention heads
  • Embedding dimensions
  • Block size (context length)
  • Optional: enable modern variants (SwiGLU, RoPE, etc.)

Step 6: Train and Monitor

Section titled “Step 6: Train and Monitor”

Start training with real-time monitoring:

  • Live loss curves
  • Sample generation at intervals
  • Perplexity tracking
  • Checkpoint management

Step 7: Inspect Results

Section titled “Step 7: Inspect Results”

After training, dive deep:

  • Attention pattern visualization
  • Token-level top-k predictions
  • Training log streaming
  • Compare multiple runs

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”
  1. Start Small - Begin with tiny models (6 layers, 6 heads) to iterate quickly
  2. Version Everything - Use the manifest system to track experiments
  3. Monitor Closely - Check loss curves and samples during training
  4. Compare Runs - Use versioned configs for ablation studies
  5. Cache Tokens - Enable token cache to avoid re-tokenizing large datasets

Precautions, FAQ, and Troubleshooting

Section titled “Precautions, FAQ, and Troubleshooting”

Important Considerations

Section titled “Important Considerations”

⚠️ Not for Large-Scale Training

llm-madness is designed for small, reproducible experiments. It’s not suitable for:

  • Training models with billions of parameters
  • Distributed training across multiple GPUs
  • Production-scale inference

⚠️ GPU Memory Limits

Even “small” models require significant GPU memory:

  • A 6-layer, 6-head model may need 4-8GB VRAM
  • Consider reducing batch size or model dimensions if OOM occurs
  • CPU training is supported but much slower

⚠️ Training Data Quality

The quality of your output depends entirely on your training data:

  • Clean and preprocess text before training
  • Remove duplicates and low-quality content
  • Balance your dataset if training on multiple sources

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: What model sizes can I train?

A: llm-madness is optimized for models in the 1M-100M parameter range. Larger models may work but will be slow without significant GPU resources.

Q: Can I use pre-trained tokenizers?

A: The toolkit focuses on training custom BPE tokenizers, but you can modify the code to load pre-trained tokenizers like GPT-2’s.

Q: How do I enable GPU acceleration?

A: Install PyTorch with CUDA support. The toolkit automatically detects and uses CUDA if available, falling back to MPS (Apple Silicon) or CPU.

Q: Can I fine-tune existing models?

A: llm-madness is designed for training from scratch. For fine-tuning, consider tools like Hugging Face Transformers.

Q: What are the modern architecture flags?

A:

  • use_rmsnorm - RMSNorm instead of LayerNorm
  • use_swiglu - Gated FFN (SwiGLU) instead of GELU
  • use_rope - Rotary position embeddings
  • use_sdpa - Scaled dot-product attention (CUDA only)
  • use_kv_cache - Faster autoregressive generation

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”
IssueSolution
CUDA out of memoryReduce batch size or model dimensions
Slow trainingEnable GPU acceleration or use smaller models
Poor generation qualityTrain longer, use more data, or adjust temperature
Tokenizer errorsCheck input file encoding and format
Web UI not loadingEnsure all dependencies are installed

Technical Deep Dive

Section titled “Technical Deep Dive”

Model Architecture

Section titled “Model Architecture”

The core GPT implementation includes:

Training Loop Features

Section titled “Training Loop Features”

Configuration System

Section titled “Configuration System”

All settings are managed through JSON configs with CLI override support:

Experiment Tracking

Section titled “Experiment Tracking”

Every run generates a run.json manifest containing:

  • Input configurations and hyperparameters
  • Output artifacts and checkpoints
  • Git commit SHA for reproducibility
  • Training metrics and samples
  • Complete provenance tracking

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
ToolFocusWeb UILocal TrainingCustom Tokenizer
llm-madnessSmall GPT training✅✅✅
Hugging Face TransformersPre-trained models❌⚠️⚠️
nanoGPTEducational GPT❌✅⚠️
lit-gptProduction LLMs❌✅❌
llm.cRaw C implementation❌✅❌

Key Differentiators

Section titled “Key Differentiators”
  1. Web-Based Experiment Management - Unique among GPT training tools
  2. Integrated Tokenizer Training - BPE training is first-class
  3. Reproducibility Focus - SHA-256 hashing and manifest tracking
  4. Educational Design - Clean code for learning transformers

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”

Official Resources

Section titled “Official Resources”
  • GitHub Repository: MaxHastings/llm-madness
  • Issues & Discussions: GitHub Issues

Related Projects

Section titled “Related Projects”
  • nanoGPT - Andrej Karpathy’s educational GPT implementation
  • llm.c - GPT training in raw C
  • lit-gpt - Production-ready LLM training

Learning Resources

Section titled “Learning Resources”
  • Attention Is All You Need - Original transformer paper
  • The Illustrated Transformer - Visual guide to transformers
  • GPT-3 Paper - Language models are few-shot learners

Summary: llm-madness fills an important niche in the LLM ecosystem—a lightweight, accessible toolkit for training small GPT models locally. Whether you’re learning about transformers, prototyping a domain-specific model, or conducting architecture research, it provides a complete pipeline without the complexity of enterprise ML platforms. The web-based UI and experiment tracking make it particularly valuable for iterative experimentation and educational exploration.

Tags:
  • AI Development
  • LLM Training
  • Open Source
  • Machine Learning
  • GPT
  • Transformer

Oh My OpenCode: Claude Code on Steroids - The Ultimate AI Agent Harness

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

In the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development tools, OpenCode has emerged as a formidable open-source alternative to commercial AI coding assistants. But if you’re a hacker who loves customization and maximum control, Oh My OpenCode is the plugin that transforms OpenCode into something extraordinary.

Created by developer code-yeongyu after spending $24,000 worth of tokens on personal development with various AI tools, Oh My OpenCode represents the distillation of countless hours of testing, configuration, and real-world usage. It’s described by its author as “OpenCode on steroids” — and for good reason.

“If Claude Code does in 7 days what a human does in 3 months, Sisyphus does it in 1 hour. It just works until the task is done. It is a disciplined agent.” — B, Quant Researcher

What is Oh My OpenCode?

Section titled “What is Oh My OpenCode?”

Oh My OpenCode is a comprehensive plugin for OpenCode that adds:

  • Async Subagents — Run multiple AI agents in parallel, just like Claude Code
  • Curated Agents — Pre-configured specialist agents with optimal models
  • Crafted Tools — LSP and AST grep tools for powerful code manipulation
  • Curated MCPs — Built-in Model Context Protocol servers for extended capabilities
  • Claude Code Compatibility — Full compatibility layer for Claude Code configs

The plugin is battle-tested in production environments and has already helped developers accomplish remarkable feats:

“Knocked out 8000 eslint warnings with Oh My Opencode, just in a day” — Jacob Ferrari

“I converted a 45k line tauri app into a SaaS web app overnight using Ohmyopencode and ralph loop.” — James Hargis

Meet Sisyphus: The Default Agent

Section titled “Meet Sisyphus: The Default Agent”

At the heart of Oh My OpenCode is Sisyphus, the default orchestrator agent named after the Greek mythological figure condemned to roll a boulder up a hill for eternity.

“In greek mythology, Sisyphus was condemned to roll a boulder up a hill for eternity as punishment for deceiving the gods. LLM Agents haven’t really done anything wrong, yet they too roll their ‘stones’—their thoughts—every single day. My life is no different. Looking back, we are not so different from these agents.”

Sisyphus’s Toolkit

Section titled “Sisyphus’s Toolkit”

Sisyphus comes equipped with a powerful set of tools and teammates:

Teammates (Curated Agents):

  • Oracle (GPT 5.2 Medium) — Design and debugging
  • Frontend UI/UX Engineer (Gemini 3 Pro) — Frontend development
  • Librarian (Claude Sonnet 4.5) — Official docs and codebase exploration
  • Explore (Grok Code) — Fast codebase exploration with contextual grep

Built-in Tools:

  • Full LSP/AstGrep support for decisive refactoring
  • Todo Continuation Enforcer — Forces agents to finish tasks
  • Comment Checker — Prevents excessive AI-generated comments
  • Claude Code Compatibility — Commands, agents, skills, MCPs, and hooks
  • Curated MCPs: Exa (web search), Context7 (official docs), Grep.app (GitHub search)
  • Async agents with background execution
  • Session management and recovery

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

1. Multi-Agent Orchestration

Section titled “1. Multi-Agent Orchestration”

Oh My OpenCode enables sophisticated workflows where different AI models work in parallel:

2. LSP and AST Tools

Section titled “2. LSP and AST Tools”

Unlike other AI harnesses that limit agent access to code analysis, Oh My OpenCode gives agents full access to:

  • lsp_hover — Type info and documentation
  • lsp_goto_definition — Jump to symbol definitions
  • lsp_find_references — Find all usages across workspace
  • lsp_document_symbols — Get file symbol outline
  • lsp_workspace_symbols — Search symbols by name
  • lsp_diagnostics — Get errors before building
  • lsp_rename — Rename symbols across workspace
  • ast_grep_search — AST-aware code pattern search
  • ast_grep_replace — AST-aware code replacement

3. Ralph Loop

Section titled “3. Ralph Loop”

Inspired by Anthropic’s Ralph Wiggum plugin, the Ralph Loop is a self-referential development loop that runs until task completion:

The loop detects <promise>DONE</promise> to know when complete and auto-continues if the agent stops prematurely. It supports all programming languages and can be configured with custom iteration limits.

4. Claude Code Compatibility

Section titled “4. Claude Code Compatibility”

Oh My OpenCode provides a comprehensive compatibility layer for Claude Code:

  • Hooks Integration — PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop
  • Config Loaders — Commands, Skills, Agents, MCP configs from standard locations
  • Data Storage — Todo management and transcripts in Claude Code format
  • Compatibility Toggles — Fine-grained control over which features to enable

5. Context Management

Section titled “5. Context Management”

Intelligent context management features:

  • Context Window Monitor — Alerts at 70%+ usage
  • Preemptive Compaction — Compacts session at 85% usage (enabled by default)
  • Anthropic Auto Compact — Automatic summarization at token limits
  • Tool Output Truncator — Prevents one search from eating your entire context

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Quick Install (Interactive)

Section titled “Quick Install (Interactive)”

Follow the prompts to configure your Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini subscriptions.

Agent-Assisted Install

Section titled “Agent-Assisted Install”

Paste this into a fresh OpenCode session:

The agent will guide you through the entire setup process.

Configuration

Section titled “Configuration”

Configuration is stored in JSON/JSONC format:

User config: ~/.config/opencode/oh-my-opencode.json Project config: .opencode/oh-my-opencode.json

Example Configuration

Section titled “Example Configuration”

Usage Modes

Section titled “Usage Modes”

Keywords for Specialized Modes

Section titled “Keywords for Specialized Modes”

Include these keywords in your prompts to activate specialized modes:

  • ultrawork / ulw — Maximum performance with parallel agent orchestration
  • search / find — Maximized search effort with parallel explore and librarian agents
  • analyze / investigate — Deep analysis mode with multi-phase expert consultation

Calling Specialized Agents

Section titled “Calling Specialized Agents”

Comparison with Claude Code

Section titled “Comparison with Claude Code”
FeatureClaude CodeOh My OpenCode
Multi-model supportClaude onlyClaude, GPT, Gemini, Grok
Async agentsYesYes
LSP toolsAnalysis onlyFull refactoring
HooksYesYes (compatible)
Custom agentsSkills systemCurated + custom
ExtensibilityLimitedHighly extensible
Open sourceNoYes

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

1. Start Simple

Section titled “1. Start Simple”

If the configuration seems overwhelming, just remember: include the word ultrawork in your prompt. The agent will figure out the rest.

2. Use Background Agents for Parallel Work

Section titled “2. Use Background Agents for Parallel Work”

Let different models work on different parts simultaneously:

3. Leverage Context Management

Section titled “3. Leverage Context Management”

Oh My OpenCode aggressively uses multiple agents to lighten context load. Your agent becomes the dev team lead, and you’re the AI manager.

4. Customize for Your Workflow

Section titled “4. Customize for Your Workflow”

You can disable features you don’t need:

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Issue: Bunx fails on Ubuntu/Debian

Section titled “Issue: Bunx fails on Ubuntu/Debian”

If you installed Bun via Snap, bunx will fail due to sandboxing. Use npx instead or reinstall Bun via the official installer.

Issue: OpenCode version compatibility

Section titled “Issue: OpenCode version compatibility”

Ensure OpenCode version is 1.0.150 or higher:

Issue: Authentication errors

Section titled “Issue: Authentication errors”

For Google Gemini, consider using the external opencode-antigravity-auth plugin for multi-account load balancing.

FAQ

Section titled “FAQ”

Q: Do I need all AI subscriptions?

A: No! You can use Oh My OpenCode with just one provider. The plugin supports Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini, but you pick what you have.

Q: Is this suitable for beginners?

A: The plugin is powerful but can be complex. The author recommends including ultrawork in your prompts — the agent will handle optimization automatically.

Q: How does this compare to AmpCode?

A: Oh My OpenCode is heavily influenced by AmpCode and Claude Code, with features ported and often improved. It’s designed for the open-source OpenCode platform.

Q: Can I use this with existing Claude Code configs?

A: Yes! The Claude Code compatibility layer means your existing configurations, hooks, and skills should work out of the box.

Community and Resources

Section titled “Community and Resources”
  • GitHub Repository: code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode
  • OpenCode Documentation: opencode.ai/docs
  • Video Review: Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane
  • Medium Article: A Deep Dive into Oh My OpenCode
  • Reddit Community: r/opencodeCLI

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Oh My OpenCode represents the culmination of extensive real-world testing and refinement. It transforms OpenCode from a capable AI coding assistant into a sophisticated multi-agent orchestration platform.

Whether you’re a seasoned developer looking to maximize productivity or an AI enthusiast exploring the boundaries of what’s possible, Oh My OpenCode offers a powerful, extensible, and thoroughly tested solution.

As the author puts it:

“Stop agonizing over agent harness choices. I’ll do the research, borrow from the best, and ship updates here.”

Ready to supercharge your OpenCode experience? Install Oh My OpenCode and let Sisyphus keep that boulder rolling.

Sources:

  • Oh My OpenCode GitHub Repository
  • OpenCode Official Site
  • YouTube: Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane
  • Medium: A Deep Dive into Oh My OpenCode
Tags:
  • AI
  • Claude Code
  • OpenCode
  • AI Agents
  • Developer Tools
  • Multi-Agent
  • LSP
  • MCP

Pulse: OpenTelemetry-Native Digital Experience Platform for Real-Time User Monitoring

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Pulse is an open-source digital experience platform that combines behavioral, technical, and business insights to drive better user experiences. Built on OpenTelemetry standards with native mobile SDKs for Android and React Native, real-time monitoring, and self-hosted deployment—giving you complete control over your observability data. Here’s everything you need to know in 5 minutes.

The Modern Observability Challenge

Section titled “The Modern Observability Challenge”

Traditional monitoring tools tell you when your servers are down. APM solutions track API response times. But what about the actual user experience?

Enter Pulse—a platform that monitors every screen, interaction, and API call to give you insights into what your users actually experience in real time. It’s like having a microscope on user behavior combined with X-ray vision into your application’s performance.

1-Minute Knowledge Cards

Section titled “1-Minute Knowledge Cards”
ConceptWhat It Means
Digital Experience PlatformMonitors user behavior + technical performance + business metrics in one view
OpenTelemetry NativeBuilt on OTel standards for vendor-neutral, portable observability
Critical InteractionsTrack the user journeys that matter most—checkout flows, login sequences, content engagement
App VitalsCrash/ANR tracking, frozen/slow frame detection, network performance monitoring
Self-HostedDeploy on your infrastructure with full data ownership (MIT licensed)

Main Content

Section titled “Main Content”

Core Highlights

Section titled “Core Highlights”

1. Real-Time User Experience Monitoring

Unlike traditional monitoring that focuses on backend metrics, Pulse captures the actual user experience:

  • Screen-by-screen health tracking
  • User experience scoring across segments
  • Real-time visualization of performance degradation

2. OpenTelemetry Native Architecture

Built from the ground up on OpenTelemetry standards:

  • Vendor-neutral data collection
  • Portable telemetry that works across platforms
  • Compatible with 90+ observability tools
  • Future-proof observability investment

3. Mobile-First SDKs

Native support for mobile applications:

  • Android SDK (pulse-android-otel): OpenTelemetry instrumentation for Android apps
  • React Native SDK (pulse-react-native-otel): Cross-platform mobile monitoring
  • Automatic instrumentation with minimal configuration

4. Intelligent Alerting

Get notified when it matters:

  • Customizable threshold-based alerts
  • Multi-channel notifications (email, Slack, webhook)
  • Alert history with trend analysis
  • Context-rich notifications for faster debugging

5. ClickHouse-Powered Analytics

High-performance analytics backend:

  • Sub-second queries on massive datasets
  • Real-time aggregations and filtering
  • Efficient time-series data handling
  • Scalable architecture for growing platforms

Technical Architecture

Section titled “Technical Architecture”

Backend Stack

  • Java 17 with Vert.x for high-performance async I/O
  • MySQL for transactional data storage
  • ClickHouse for analytics and time-series queries
  • OpenTelemetry Collector for telemetry ingestion

Frontend Stack

  • React 18 with TypeScript
  • Mantine UI for modern, accessible components
  • Real-time updates via WebSocket connections

Mobile SDKs

  • Kotlin for Android with OpenTelemetry SDK
  • React Native for cross-platform support
  • OTLP protocol for standard telemetry export

Key Features Deep Dive

Section titled “Key Features Deep Dive”

Critical Interactions Tracking

Section titled “Critical Interactions Tracking”

Not all user actions are equal. Pulse lets you monitor the interactions that drive your business:

App Vitals Monitoring

Section titled “App Vitals Monitoring”

Understand application health at a glance:

  • Crash-free users: Track stability across versions
  • ANR (Application Not Responding): Detect UI freezes
  • Frame rendering: Identify slow/frozen frames affecting UX
  • Network performance: Monitor API latency and failure rates

User Growth KPIs

Section titled “User Growth KPIs”

Segment your users by experience quality:

Identify problem segments by device, region, OS version, or custom attributes.

Three-Minute Setup

Section titled “Three-Minute Setup”

Prerequisites

  • Docker and Docker Compose installed
  • 4GB RAM minimum (8GB recommended for production)

Quick Start

Mobile SDK Integration

Android (Kotlin)

React Native

Deployment Options

Section titled “Deployment Options”
OptionBest ForCost
Local DockerDevelopment & testingFree
Self-HostedProduction with data controlInfrastructure costs only
Cloud (Coming Soon)Quick setup without opsTBD

Pro Tips

Section titled “Pro Tips”
  • Start with Critical Paths: Don’t try to monitor everything. Identify your top 3 business-critical user journeys and instrument those first.
  • Use Custom Attributes: Enrich telemetry with user segments, subscription tiers, or any business-relevant metadata for powerful filtering.
  • Set Meaningful Thresholds: Alert thresholds should reflect actual user impact, not arbitrary technical metrics.
  • Correlate with Business Metrics: Pulse is most powerful when combined with revenue, retention, or conversion data.
  • Leverage OpenTelemetry: Since Pulse uses OTel, you can export to multiple backends simultaneously—Pulse for UX monitoring, Grafana for infrastructure, etc.

Comparison with Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Alternatives”
FeaturePulseFirebaseDatadogNew Relic
Open SourceYes (Apache 2.0)NoNoNo
Self-HostedYesNoNoNo
OpenTelemetry NativeYesPartialYesYes
Mobile SDKsAndroid, RNAndroid, iOS, FlutterAndroid, iOS, RNAll major
Real-Time UX MonitoringYesLimitedYesYes
ClickHouse BackendYesNoNoNo
PricingFree (self-host)Free tier + paidUsage-basedUsage-based
Data OwnershipFullGoogle controlsVendor controlsVendor controls

Coming Soon Features

Section titled “Coming Soon Features”

The Pulse roadmap includes exciting capabilities:

  • AI Insights: Auto-discover patterns and predict issues
  • Anomaly Detection: Identify unusual patterns before user impact
  • Path Analysis: Visualize user journeys and drop-off points
  • Heat Maps: See where users interact most
  • Session Replay: Watch user sessions for debugging

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Pulse represents the next generation of observability—where user experience meets technical performance, all built on open standards and self-hosted privacy.

Perfect for:

  • Streaming platforms monitoring quality during peak events
  • E-commerce apps tracking checkout flows
  • Fintech apps ensuring transaction reliability
  • Any application where user experience = business success

Repository: dream-horizon-org/pulse Documentation: pulse.dreamhorizon.org

Quick Social Shares

Section titled “Quick Social Shares”

  1. “Just discovered Pulse—an open-source digital experience platform built on OpenTelemetry. Real-time user monitoring, mobile SDKs, self-hosted. Like Datadog meets Firebase but you own your data.”

  2. “Need mobile observability that doesn’t cost a fortune? Pulse uses ClickHouse for analytics, has native Android/React Native SDKs, and is completely self-hosted. Apache 2.0 licensed.”

  3. “The future of observability is here: monitor user experience, app vitals, and critical interactions in one platform. OpenTelemetry-native, self-hosted, and free. Check out Pulse by dream-horizon-org.”

Want to see a detailed integration guide or comparison with other APM tools? Let me know in the comments!

Sources:

  • dream-horizon-org/pulse GitHub Repository
  • Pulse Official Documentation
  • OpenTelemetry Documentation
Tags:
  • Observability
  • OpenTelemetry
  • Pulse
  • Digital Experience
  • Mobile Monitoring
  • Self-Hosted

Second Brain AI Assistant: Open-Source Course for Building Agentic RAG Systems

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Explore Decoding ML’s open-source course that teaches you how to build a production-ready Second Brain AI assistant using LLMs, RAG, agents, and LLMOps best practices.

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

Imagine asking your AI assistant to recommend agent courses, list top PDF parsing tools, or summarize LLM optimization methods—all based on your personal research, without manually searching through your notes. This is the promise of the Second Brain AI Assistant concept.

Created by Paul Iusztin and the Decoding ML team, this open-source course teaches you how to build a production-ready agentic RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and LLM system from scratch.

Repository: decodingai-magazine/second-brain-ai-assistant-course

Course Series: Build Your Second Brain AI Assistant

What is a Second Brain AI Assistant?

Section titled “What is a Second Brain AI Assistant?”

The Second Brain concept, popularized by Tiago Forte, refers to your personal knowledge base of notes, ideas, and resources. A Second Brain AI Assistant leverages this knowledge to:

  • Answer questions based on your research
  • Summarize documents from your knowledge base
  • Provide insights by connecting your notes
  • Recommend resources you’ve curated

While the course uses Notion as the data source, the architecture is adaptable to other sources like Google Drive, Calendar, or custom databases.

Course Overview

Section titled “Course Overview”

This comprehensive course contains 6 modules covering theory, system design, and hands-on implementation:

ModuleTopicCode
0Modules OverviewNo code
1Build your Second Brain AI assistantNo code
2Data pipelines for AI assistantsapps/second-brain-offline
3From noisy docs to fine-tuning datasetsapps/second-brain-offline
4Playbook to fine-tune and deploy LLMsapps/second-brain-offline
5Build RAG pipelines that actually workapps/second-brain-offline
6LLMOps for production agentic RAGapps/second-brain-online

Key Features

Section titled “Key Features”

Production-Ready Architecture

Section titled “Production-Ready Architecture”

The course teaches you to build:

  • Agentic RAG System: Advanced retrieval with contextual and parent document retrieval
  • Production LLM Architecture: Scalable design using FTI (Features, Training, Inference) principles
  • Observability Pipeline: Monitor and evaluate AI assistant performance

Modern Tooling Stack

Section titled “Modern Tooling Stack”
CategoryTools
LLM APIsOpenAI, Hugging Face
Vector DatabaseMongoDB
Pipeline OrchestrationZenML
LLMOps & EvaluationOpik
Experiment TrackingComet
Fine-tuningUnsloth
Agent Frameworksmolagents
Python Toolinguv, ruff

What You’ll Learn

Section titled “What You’ll Learn”

By completing this course, you’ll master:

  1. LLM System Architecture - FTI methodology and MLOps best practices
  2. Pipeline Orchestration - Tracking with ZenML
  3. LLMOps & RAG Evaluation - Using Opik
  4. Large-scale Web Crawling - Content normalization
  5. Quality Scoring - LLMs and heuristics
  6. Dataset Generation - Through distillation
  7. Llama Model Fine-tuning - With Unsloth and Comet
  8. Serverless Model Deployment - To Hugging Face
  9. Advanced RAG Techniques - Contextual retrieval, parent retrieval, vector search
  10. Agent Building - Using smolagents
  11. Modern Python Tooling - uv and ruff

Prerequisites & Cost

Section titled “Prerequisites & Cost”

Prerequisites

Section titled “Prerequisites”
CategoryRequirements
SkillsPython (Intermediate), ML/LLMs/RAG (Beginner)
HardwareModern laptop/PC (GPU optional - cloud alternatives provided)
LevelIntermediate (accessible with patience)

Cost Structure

Section titled “Cost Structure”

The course is completely FREE and open-source!

If you choose to run the code, costs are minimal:

ServiceMaximum Cost
OpenAI API~$3
Hugging Face Endpoints (optional)~$2

Reading-only? Everything is 100% free!

Project Structure

Section titled “Project Structure”

The course builds two separate Python applications:

  • second-brain-offline: Data pipelines, RAG indexing, fine-tuning
  • second-brain-online: Real-time inference API with observability

Course Sponsors

Section titled “Course Sponsors”

This open-source course is supported by industry leaders:

Who Should Take This Course?

Section titled “Who Should Take This Course?”
Target AudienceWhy Join?
ML/AI EngineersBuild production-ready agentic RAG & LLM systems (beyond notebook tutorials)
Data/Software EngineersArchitect end-to-end agentic RAG and LLM applications
Data ScientistsImplement production AI systems using LLMOps and SWE best practices

This course is tailored for people who learn by building. After completion, you’ll have your own code template and inspiration to develop personal GenAI applications.

Getting Started

Section titled “Getting Started”

  1. Clone the repository:

  2. Read the course materials on Decoding ML

  3. Set up the code following the documentation in each app

  4. Run and replicate the results

  5. Go deeper into the code to understand implementation details

Core Contributors

Section titled “Core Contributors”
Paul Iusztin
AI/ML Engineer		Ernesto Larios
AI Engineer		Anca Ioana Muscalagiu
SWE/ML Engineer

Related Resources

Section titled “Related Resources”
  • Decoding ML Newsletter: https://decodingml.substack.com/
  • From 0 to Pro: AI Engineering Roadmap: Read Guide
  • GitHub Issues: Questions & Support

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

The Second Brain AI Assistant course is an exceptional free resource for anyone serious about building production-grade RAG and LLM systems. By combining theoretical knowledge with hands-on implementation, it bridges the gap between notebook tutorials and real-world applications.

Whether you’re an ML engineer looking to level up your RAG skills or a software engineer wanting to architect AI applications, this course provides the practical foundation you need.

License: MIT License - Free to use and modify

Have you built your Second Brain AI assistant? Share your experience and join the discussion at the GitHub repository.

Tags:
  • RAG
  • LLM
  • Agents
  • LLMOps
  • Fine-tuning
  • Python
  • Decoding ML
  • Open Source
  • Course

SivaLabs Marketplace: Supercharge Spring Boot Development with Claude Code

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Introduction

Section titled “Introduction”

Claude Code has rapidly become a powerful AI-powered development companion, and its extensibility through plugins and MCP servers opens up incredible possibilities for specialized development workflows. Enter SivaLabs Marketplace - a curated collection of plugins designed specifically for Java and Spring Boot developers.

Created by K. Siva Prasad Reddy (sivalabs), a well-known Spring Boot author and educator, this marketplace brings enterprise Java development patterns directly into your AI-assisted coding experience.

What is SivaLabs Marketplace?

Section titled “What is SivaLabs Marketplace?”

SivaLabs Marketplace is a plugin collection for Claude Code that focuses on Spring Boot application development using the Java, Spring Boot, and Spring Data JPA technology stack. It provides specialized skills that automate common development tasks while following established best practices and architectural patterns.

The marketplace is currently in active development (as of January 2026) and includes the spring-boot-dev plugin as its flagship offering.

Installation

Section titled “Installation”

Getting started with SivaLabs Marketplace is straightforward:

After installation, you can verify the plugin and its skills are available using the /plugins command in Claude CLI.

Available Skills

Section titled “Available Skills”

The spring-boot-dev plugin includes five specialized skills that streamline Spring Boot development:

1. spring-boot-package-structure-creator

Section titled “1. spring-boot-package-structure-creator”

Creates the recommended package structure for Spring Boot projects following best practices. This skill ensures your project starts with a solid architectural foundation.

2. jpa-entity-creator

Section titled “2. jpa-entity-creator”

Automates the creation of JPA entities with proper annotations, relationships, and best practices. Simply describe your entity requirements, and the skill generates production-ready entity classes.

3. spring-data-jpa-repo-creator

Section titled “3. spring-data-jpa-repo-creator”

Generates Spring Data JPA repository interfaces for your entities, including custom query methods when needed.

4. spring-service-creator

Section titled “4. spring-service-creator”

Creates Spring service layer classes with proper dependency injection, transaction management, and business logic separation.

5. spring-rest-api-creator

Section titled “5. spring-rest-api-creator”

Builds Spring MVC REST API controllers with appropriate annotations, request/response handling, and validation.

Practical Example

Section titled “Practical Example”

Here’s how you can use SivaLabs Marketplace to quickly build a CRUD REST API:

  1. Create a Spring Boot project with Web, Validation, Spring Data JPA, and H2 dependencies
  2. In Claude CLI, prompt: “Implement CRUD REST API for Person with id, name, email and phone fields”
  3. The plugin will automatically generate:
    • JPA Entity (Person.java)
    • Repository interface (PersonRepository.java)
    • Service layer (PersonService.java)
    • REST Controller (PersonController.java)

Each generated component follows Spring Boot best practices and integrates seamlessly with the others.

Included MCP Server

Section titled “Included MCP Server”

The marketplace also includes the context7 MCP server, which provides general library documentation lookup capabilities. This enhances Claude Code’s ability to reference official Spring documentation and other libraries during development.

Best Practices and Use Cases

Section titled “Best Practices and Use Cases”

When to Use SivaLabs Marketplace

Section titled “When to Use SivaLabs Marketplace”
  • New Spring Boot Projects: Quickly scaffold properly structured projects
  • CRUD Operations: Generate standard create, read, update, delete APIs
  • Learning Spring Boot: See best practices in generated code
  • Prototyping: Rapidly build working APIs for testing and validation

Tips for Effective Use

Section titled “Tips for Effective Use”
  1. Start with Package Structure: Use spring-boot-package-structure-creator first to establish your project layout
  2. Describe Entities Clearly: When using jpa-entity-creator, provide detailed field specifications including types, constraints, and relationships
  3. Review Generated Code: While the plugins follow best practices, always review and adjust generated code to match your specific requirements
  4. Use Context7: Leverage the included MCP server for accessing up-to-date Spring documentation

Comparison with Similar Tools

Section titled “Comparison with Similar Tools”
FeatureSivaLabs MarketplaceGeneric AI CodingManual Development
Spring Boot Best Practices✓ Built-inVariableDepends on developer
Package Structure✓ AutomatedOften missingManual setup
Integration Patterns✓ StandardizedInconsistentManual implementation
Learning CurveLow for Spring devsVariesHigh for beginners
CustomizationGenerated code is editableRequires promptsFull control

Precautions and Limitations

Section titled “Precautions and Limitations”
  • Early Development: The marketplace is actively developed; features may evolve
  • Spring Boot Specific: Not suitable for non-Spring Java projects
  • Code Review Required: Always review generated code for security, performance, and business logic
  • Claude Code Dependency: Requires Claude Code CLI environment

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Plugin Not Found

Section titled “Plugin Not Found”
  • Ensure the marketplace is added and enabled
  • Check your internet connection for marketplace access
  • Verify Claude Code is up to date

Generated Code Issues

Section titled “Generated Code Issues”
  • Provide more specific prompts with detailed requirements
  • Review the Spring Boot version compatibility
  • Check that all required dependencies are in your pom.xml or build.gradle

Future Developments

Section titled “Future Developments”

Given K. Siva Prasad Reddy’s expertise in Spring Boot and his active YouTube channel featuring Spring Boot tutorials, we can expect:

  • Additional skills for testing (JUnit, Mockito, Testcontainers)
  • Security configuration generators
  • Docker and Kubernetes deployment helpers
  • Integration with other Spring ecosystem projects

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

SivaLabs Marketplace represents an exciting evolution in AI-assisted Spring Boot development. By combining Claude Code’s powerful AI capabilities with specialized Spring Boot knowledge, it helps developers work faster while maintaining code quality and following established patterns.

Whether you’re a seasoned Spring Boot developer looking to accelerate workflow or a learning developer wanting to see best practices in action, SivaLabs Marketplace is a valuable addition to your Claude Code toolkit.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • SivaLabs Marketplace GitHub Repository
  • K. Siva Prasad Reddy (Author)
  • Build a Spring Boot App with Claude Code AI - YouTube Tutorial
  • Spring Boot Official Documentation
  • Claude Code Documentation

Sources:

  • SivaLabs Marketplace GitHub
  • Siva (@sivalabs) on X
Tags:
  • Claude Code
  • Java
  • Spring Boot
  • AI Development
  • MCP Servers
  • Plugins

Slash Commands Collection: 13 AI-Powered Developer Productivity Commands

Jan 4, 2026
Vibe Sparking

As AI coding assistants become essential tools for modern developers, the ability to customize and streamline their behavior through slash commands has emerged as a powerful productivity booster. The slash-commands collection by regenrek offers a curated set of 13 battle-tested commands designed specifically for code review, problem analysis, and refactoring workflows. Let’s explore this comprehensive toolkit that works across Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.

What is the Slash Commands Collection?

Section titled “What is the Slash Commands Collection?”

The slash-commands repository is a carefully curated collection of AI-powered slash commands created by Kevin Kern, a developer focused on building tools that enhance AI-assisted coding workflows. The collection addresses common pain points developers face when working with AI coding assistants:

  • Code review consistency - Standardized reviews across team members
  • Problem diagnosis - Systematic bug identification and impact analysis
  • Refactoring workflows - Structured approaches to code improvements
  • Planning and organization - Task management and documentation practices

Each command is designed as a Markdown template that can be invoked with a simple /command-name syntax, making complex workflows repeatable and shareable across teams.

Installation Guide

Section titled “Installation Guide”

For Claude Code Users

Section titled “For Claude Code Users”

Claude Code supports both project-specific and personal commands:

Project commands (shared with your team):

Personal commands (available across all projects):

For Cursor Users

Section titled “For Cursor Users”

For Codex Users

Section titled “For Codex Users”

Complete Command Reference

Section titled “Complete Command Reference”

1. /create-plan - Comprehensive Task Planning

Section titled “1. /create-plan - Comprehensive Task Planning”

Purpose: Creates structured implementation plans with all necessary sections before development begins.

Usage:

Features:

  • Context and problem statement
  • Measurable success criteria
  • Clear deliverables and approach
  • Risk assessment and testing strategy
  • Cross-references to AGENTS.md for rules/constraints

When to use: At the start of new features or complex tasks to ensure comprehensive planning.

2. /plan-review - Implementation Plan Review

Section titled “2. /plan-review - Implementation Plan Review”

Purpose: Reviews implementation plans and provides a go/no-go decision for development.

Usage:

Evaluates:

  • Codebase alignment and current patterns
  • Scope clarity and completeness
  • Performance, security, and privacy impact
  • Code smells and potential caveats

When to use: Before starting complex implementations to validate your approach.

3. /finalize-plan - Task Completion Documentation

Section titled “3. /finalize-plan - Task Completion Documentation”

Purpose: Finalizes completed tasks by creating finished plan files and lightweight summaries.

Usage:

Features:

  • Moves task to finished directory with status
  • Creates lightweight summary file (SOW - Summary of Work)
  • Cross-references between plan and summary
  • Tracks affected files, tests, and decisions
  • Token-optimized for AI consumption

When to use: When tasks are completed to maintain organized documentation.

4. /code-review-low - Fast Code Review

Section titled “4. /code-review-low - Fast Code Review”

Purpose: Lightweight code review focusing on critical issues only.

Usage:

Focus Areas:

  • Code smells and security vulnerabilities
  • Performance bottlenecks
  • Essential test coverage

Output:

  • Summary with top 1-3 risks
  • Decision: approve | request_changes | blocker
  • Findings grouped by Smell | Security | Performance | Tests
  • Exact file and line range references

When to use: After implementing features for quick validation.

5. /code-review-high - Thorough Code Review

Section titled “5. /code-review-high - Thorough Code Review”

Purpose: Comprehensive code review with extended analysis for major changes.

Usage:

Includes: All code-review-low checks plus:

  • Deeper security and performance analysis
  • Comprehensive test recommendations
  • Architecture and design pattern review
  • Dependency and integration analysis

When to use: For major changes or before releases.

6. /problem-analyzer - Bug Identification and Impact Analysis

Section titled “6. /problem-analyzer - Bug Identification and Impact Analysis”

Purpose: Identifies bugs and maps affected files across the codebase.

Usage:

Provides:

  • Root cause analysis
  • File impact assessment
  • Minimal safe fix proposals
  • Documentation gap identification

Pro tip: Works best when combined with error logs and a short problem description. Consider using with faster models like Cheetah for quick analysis, then switching to more capable models for detailed solutions.

When to use: During debugging sessions for faster issue resolution.

7. /refactor-code - Structured Refactoring Workflows

Section titled “7. /refactor-code - Structured Refactoring Workflows”

Purpose: Initiates refactoring workflows with clear scope and isolation.

Usage:

Features:

  • Feature-specific refactoring scope
  • Commit isolation guidelines
  • Documentation of unrelated issues (without fixing)
  • No backward compatibility or feature flags

When to use: When starting refactoring work to maintain clean commit history.

8. /kill-port - Port Management Utility

Section titled “8. /kill-port - Port Management Utility”

Purpose: Kills processes running on specified ports with OS-specific commands.

Usage:

Features:

  • Cross-platform support (macOS, Linux, Windows)
  • Process identification and verification
  • Safety warnings for system processes
  • Graceful shutdown recommendations

When to use: When ports are occupied and need to be freed up.

9. /research-better-lib - Library Evaluation Framework

Section titled “9. /research-better-lib - Library Evaluation Framework”

Purpose: Comprehensive template for evaluating alternatives to baseline libraries.

Usage:

Features:

  • Structured problem statement and success metrics
  • Search query templates for benchmarking
  • Candidate library shortlist evaluation
  • Minimal benchmark design guidelines
  • Decision rubric and deliverables checklist

When to use: When considering library replacements to find modern, faster alternatives.

10. /ask-codefetch - Codebase Context Gathering

Section titled “10. /ask-codefetch - Codebase Context Gathering”

Purpose: Gathers narrowed codebase context into a single markdown file using codefetch.

Usage:

Workflow:

  1. Research: identify relevant files/directories for the task
  2. Generate: run codefetch with scoped files → single .md file
  3. Read: use codefetch/codebase.md as context

When to use: When you need to provide context to an AI about specific parts of your codebase.

11. /ask-codefetch-pro - Quick Codebase Analysis

Section titled “11. /ask-codefetch-pro - Quick Codebase Analysis”

Purpose: Generate codebase, copy to clipboard, and automatically open AI chat.

Usage:

When to use: For quick codebase analysis with automatic clipboard and browser integration.

12. /solidjs-rules - SolidJS Best Practices

Section titled “12. /solidjs-rules - SolidJS Best Practices”

Purpose: Comprehensive SolidJS coding rules and best practices.

Usage:

Covers:

  • Reactivity basics and signal usage
  • Control flow helpers (Show, For, Switch)
  • Async and side effect patterns
  • Component API and prop guidelines
  • Styling conventions
  • File and folder organization
  • ESLint configuration examples

When to use: When working with SolidJS codebases to ensure best practices.

13. /component-cleanup - SolidJS Component Structure

Section titled “13. /component-cleanup - SolidJS Component Structure”

Purpose: SolidJS component size and structure rules for code cleanup.

Usage:

Guidelines:

  • Component size guidelines (150-250 LOC target, 600 LOC hard cap)
  • File naming conventions
  • Component splitting strategies
  • Logic extraction to hooks/utilities
  • JSX nesting limits

When to use: When reviewing or refactoring SolidJS components.

14. /short-tokens - Token Optimization

Section titled “14. /short-tokens - Token Optimization”

Purpose: Minimizes token usage while retaining all key information.

Usage:

Principles:

  • Trim aggressively for token savings
  • Retain every key fact, figure, and conclusion
  • Remove redundancy and filler
  • Avoid ** asterisk formatting
  • Every token consumes space in agent token windows

When to use: When optimizing text content for AI token windows.

Recommended Workflow Integration

Section titled “Recommended Workflow Integration”

Daily Development Workflow

Section titled “Daily Development Workflow”
  1. Morning Planning: Use /create-plan for new tasks
  2. Pre-Implementation: Use /plan-review to validate approach
  3. During Development: Use /problem-analyzer when bugs appear
  4. Post-Implementation: Use /code-review-low for quick validation
  5. Major Milestones: Use /code-review-high before releases
  6. Task Completion: Use /finalize-plan to document outcomes

Team Collaboration

Section titled “Team Collaboration”
  • Share commands through project-level .claude/commands/ directories
  • Standardize reviews using /code-review-low or /code-review-high
  • Document decisions using /finalize-plan summaries
  • Debug together using /problem-analyzer with shared logs

SolidJS Projects

Section titled “SolidJS Projects”
  • Use /solidjs-rules for onboarding new developers
  • Apply /component-cleanup during code reviews
  • Combine both for comprehensive SolidJS quality checks

Comparison with Built-in Alternatives

Section titled “Comparison with Built-in Alternatives”
Featureregenrek slash-commandsClaude Code Built-in /review
Code Review LevelsTwo: low (fast) and high (thorough)Single level
CustomizationFully editable Markdown templatesFixed prompt
